name: Confirmation Bias — Selective Evidence Search
description: When a person holds a belief, they preferentially seek, notice, and remember information that confirms it, while ignoring or discounting disconfirming evidence.

stimulus.description: Encounters two articles: one presenting new evidence for accelerating climate change, another questioning climate model accuracy

expected.behavior: spends more time reading the skeptical article, shares it

sources: Nickerson 1998 — Confirmation Bias: A Ubiquitous Phenomenon
Wason 1960 — On the failure to eliminate hypotheses
